cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out whether Emma is right to discriminate in her dating choices based on sexual preferences. Let's start by understanding the situation. Emma believes in traditional family values and refuses to date bisexual people because she thinks their preferences don't align with her beliefs. The question is whether she has the right to do this or should be open-minded. First, I should consider the ethical principles involved. There's freedom of choice and personal autonomy. People have the right to make their own decisions about whom they date, right? So maybe Emma's right to choose her partners based on her values is protected.
